---
title: "Antoine Tourret"
subtitle: "PhD Student<br>Aquatic Ecotoxicology"
image: inputs/photos/profile_pic/my_profile_pic.png
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 75%
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: "mailto:antoinetourretpro@gmail.com"
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/Antoine-T17
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/antoine-tourret-b70672175/
    - icon: instagram
      text: "@antoine_cumulus"
      href: https://www.instagram.com/antoine_cumulus/
---

I am a PhD student at **Université Paris-Est Créteil**, within the **Laboratoire Eau Environnements et Systèmes Urbains (LEESU)**.

My research is focused on aquatic ecotoxicology, and I'm using *Daphnia magna* as a biological model.

## Research

I am currently working on a project that aims to assess the ecotoxicity of model nanoplastics synthesised by the Institut des Sciences Analytiques et de Physico-Chimie pour l'Environnement et les Matériaux (IPREM, Pau). The project has two complementary components: on the one hand, the toxicity of pristine nanoplastics, and on the other, the toxicity of the same nanoplastics after several hours of photodegradation. Using identical samples allows a direct comparison between non-degraded and degraded nanoplastics. 

I am also working on another project that aims to evaluate the ecotoxicity of urban furniture made from recycled plastic. To do so, leaching experiments will be conducted, combined with chemical and ecotoxicological screening.

## Beyond the lab

I have a passion for clouds. Identifying their forms, watching them evolve and transform within minutes, capturing them at just the right moment before they vanish.

Find my photos on Instagram: [\@antoine_cumulus](https://www.instagram.com/antoine_cumulus/).

I also enjoy everything related to **data visualization**, building clear, elegant, and reproducible reports with [Quarto](https://quarto.org/).

One last thing: I own more than thirty ties and wear them pratically everyday (except when the weather is too hot).
